calendar.matters:
Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Nov-17-2020 Off Calendar

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Continued To Nov-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Cross appeared by zoom for Louis DeMartini and Stephen DeMartini
(Co-Trustees).

Mr. Cross stated that Mr. Lewis dismissed the petition to suspend and remove the Co-Trustees, that Mr. Lewis is now in pro per and noted that Mr. Lewis is not here today. The Court stated that the matter is on calendar today to see what Mr. Lewis' wishes are, that it is going to continue this one more time to give Mr. Lewis the opportunity to say if he is pursuing his petition and that if not, it will take the petition off calendar at the next hearing. Mr. Cross stated that he will tell Mr. Lewis. The Petition to Suspend and Remove Co-Trustees is continued to November 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Co-Trustees Accounts And Status Reports For Calendar Years 2011 Through 2015
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Continued To Oct-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Colopy appeared by telephone for Michael Lewis (Beneficiary/Petitioner).
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for Louis DeMartini and Stephen DeMartini (Co-Trustees).
Mark Lewis (Beneficiary/Objector) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it has read everything in this case and informed the parties that the accountings will not be open forever. Mr. Cross stated that the Co-Trustees owe the Beneficiaries a 2019 accounting, that he was informed by the Accountant this morning that the accounting is completed and that they will have it, that the 2019 accounting was drafted, but did not balance and that the Paralegal is going through the statements to get it in balance. The Court noted that this accounting petition has been on for three years, stated that it does not see the 2018 and the 2019 data being relevant to this accounting, and that it will not review an accounting for a decade. Mr. Colopy provided history, stated that Judge Busch had been closely monitoring this, that one of the Beneficiaries passed away and that upon her death the trust was to wind up and terminate, that two of the three properties were sold, referred the April 15th letter from his Client to the Co-Trustees and stated that there was no response to the letter. Mr. Colopy also stated that the proceeds from the sales have not been distributed to the Beneficiaries, that the Co-Trustees have provided no copies of the sales agreements, that no information has been provided regarding compensation to the Co-Trustees and that he would like to wrap this up, but it can't be done without the information being provided. The Court informed the parties that it reads things differently than Judge Busch, stated that it will not babysit this, that the accounting will not cover the separate entity, that the issue is that the Co-Trustees invested in a poorly managed opaque non-performing asset, that the information being requested falls under discovery and that the issues of the DJA assets perhaps can be asserted as a breach of fiduciary duties. The Court also noted that the accounting period in the petition is through 2015, stated that the accounting petition is going to get resolved at the next calling of the case, that it is going to establish deadlines and that if the parties can get to resolution with the accounting, the time is now. Mr. Colopy stated that he filed objections and asked for an evidentiary hearing, and that he requested information regarding compensation to the Trustees in their dual roles of trustees of the trust and control of the joint account. Discussion was held with regard to whether or not the issue of removal/suspension is moot and whether it makes sense to remove or suspend at this time. The Parties submit to the accounting petition. The Petition for Approval of Co-Trustees Accounts and Status Reports, etc. is under submission. The Petition to Remove and Suspend Co-Trustees is continued to October 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court urges everyone to get all information to the other side promptly and suggests that the parties work towards resolution.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Co-Trustees Accounts And Status Reports For Calendar Years 2011 Through 2015
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Sep-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Sep-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Co-Trustees Accounts And Status Reports For Calendar Years 2011 Through 2015
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Continued To Jun-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Continued To Jun-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Continued To Mar-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Cross for Louis Demartini and Stephen Demartini.
James Colopy for Michael Lewis (Petitioner/Objector/Beneficiary).

Mr. Cross stated that the Paralegal is working with the Bookkeeper on the 2017 and 2018 accountings, that the Bookkeeper is not a Probate Accountant, that the Paralegal has spent several hours with the Bookkeeper, that he thinks they may have them by the end of next week and suggested a 30-day continuance. Mr. Cross also stated that they are selling the trust assets (a percentage interest in a partnership that has three properties), that two of the properties were sold, that they got an offer on the third property and that they are expecting to get it sold soon. In addition, Mr. Cross stated that he is hoping to get the trust closed by the end of the year and get distribution out to the Beneficiaries, that they made a partial distribution, that the second property sold and they will be making a further distribution with a target date of within 30-days, and that the net proceeds on the first sale was $17,800,000.00 and the second property was about $3,000,000.00. Furthermore, Mr. Cross stated that taxes had to be paid, that there are hold backs and that he will have a letter out this week explaining all of this to the Beneficiaries. Mr. Colopy stated that promises have been made and not fulfilled, that there has been no response to three letters, and that no document information has been provided. Mr. Colopy also stated that they were told that the amount of the first sale would be distributed, but 50% was held back and they don't know why, and that the Trustees stopped making income distributions over two years ago and they also don't know why. The Petition to Suspend and Remove Co-Trustees, etc. and the Petition for Approval of Co-Trustees Account, etc. are continued to March 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If possible, the Parties are to provide a joint status statement by March 17, 2020. The Court stated that Mr. Cross' Clients need to do a better job at communicating what is going on here.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Co-Trustees Accounts And Status Reports For Calendar Years 2011 Through 2015
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Continued To Mar-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Continued To Feb-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Colopy appeared by telephone for Michael Louis (Petitioner).
Robert Cross for Louis Demartini and Stephen Demartini (Respondents).

Mr. Cross stated that the life income beneficiary died, that the Trustees are working on rolling up the partnership/LLC, that there are three properties, that the primary property with the most value in Fairfield (the mall) is in contract for sale, that the second property has had some interest, but is not in contract, that the Rohnert Park property has had no interest and that they hope to sell the properties in the Spring of 2020. Mr. Cross also stated that the Trustees will have to hold back some money as part of the sale contract to indemnify the Buyer against any unforeseen problems, that some accountings are still outstanding, that they will provide them, and that the issue of whether or not this petition can go away depends on how the accountings are seen by the Petitioner and whether or not they may want to waive their objections. Mr. Colopy stated that there are issues that need to be resolved, that they don't have the accountings for 2017 and 2018, that they were told 2017 was nearly completed and that they were working on 2018, that they need to know what is in the accountings so that they can address the issue of surcharge against the Trustees for excessive compensation and that there haven't been any income distributions for a year and a half. Mr. Colopy also stated that before there were monthly distributions, that they don't know why the distributions have stopped, that they have asked and received no answer, and that without having full information his Client is not in a position to say that the petition can go away. Mr. Cross asked for 60 to 90-days to complete the accountings. The Petition to Suspend and Remove Co-Trustees and the Petition for Approval of Co-Trustees Accounts, etc. are continued to February 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to provide a joint status statement by February 18, 2020 indicating what issues in the existing petition remain alive, what the status is and how they are going to move forward. The Court expects that in the meantime the Parties will be talking to each other. Mr. Cross informed the Court that Mark is now self-represented. The Court informed Counsel that Mark needs to be part of the joint status statement.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Continued To Oct-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Colopy appeared by telephone for Michael Louis (Beneficiary).
Robert Cross for Louis DeMartini and Stepehen DeMartini (Co-Trustees/Respondents).

Mr. Cross stated that they had mediation and were going towards settlement, but the life time Beneficiary of the trust died, that his Client is in the process of liquidating the trust and that by the end of the year they can hopefully make distribution. Mr. Colopy stated that the Trustee recently sent a letter to the Beneficiaries indicating that the properties are being marketed, and asked that the matter be put over to mid October to allow the properties to be sold. The Petition to Suspend and Remover Co-Trustees, etc. and the Petition for Approval of Co-Trustees Accounts, etc. are continued to October 15, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to file a joint status statement by October 8, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Robert Cross for Louis DeMartini and Steven DeMartini

Mr. Colopy stated that Counsel have agreed to continue this matter to August 14th, and they have a mediation scheduled for June 21st. The Petition for Approval, etc., and The Petition to Suspend, etc. are continued to August 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Co-Trustees Accounts And Status Reports For Calendar Years 2011 Through 2015
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To May-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To May-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Cross and Seth Skootsky for Louis DeMartini and Stephen DeMartini (Trustees).
Jim Colopy for and with Mark Lewis and for Michael Lewis (Beneficiaries).
John Minton appeared by telephone for Laurie Low (Beneficiary).

Discussion was held regarding whether or not it makes sense to bifurcate into multiple phases, whether there is another way of proceeding, the partnership issue, the Trust, whether a judgment on the pleadings or motion for summary judgment would be filed, whether it makes sense for the Trial Judge to manage the order of the issues, etc. Counsel stated that they are not ready for trial and that no formal discovery has been conducted. Mr. Minton stated that he does not anticipate his Client coming in as a separate party and that he supports the Beneficiaries. The Petition to Suspend and Remove Co-Trustees, etc. and Petition for Approval of Co-Trustees Accounts, etc. are continued to May 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that discovery is completed and the parties are ready to set a trial date without prejudice to the parties having agreed to a different way of proceeding or bringing an appropriate motion. The parties are to meet and confer, and prepare a joint statement by May 8th addressing the length of the trial, the witnesses, any factual stipulations they can agree to, exhibits, etc. and whether Mr. Minton's position has changed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Suspend And Remove Louis C. Demartini And Stephen W. Demartini As Co-Trustees Of The Louis J. Demartini Residuary Trust And Exhibits A-C
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Continued To Jan-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Louis DeMartini and Stephen DeMartini.
James Colopy for Michael Lewis and Mark Lewis.
John Minton appeared by telephone for Laurie Low.

The Court stated that there seems to be two things: 1) a desire to be fully informed and 2) urging of diversification in holdings. Mr. Colopy stated that his Clients had not been getting any information at all for two years, that the information is not forthcoming, spoke of the partnership 50% ownership in the Trust, stated that it had been valued at $3,000,000.00 for years and years, that the net income is dramatically down, that liability is dramatically up and that the working cap is dramatically down. Mr. Colopy also stated that his Clients need an accurate valuation of the Trust ownership, information on any compensation they are receiving (about half a million a year from the Trust), and that there are serious problems. Mr. Skootsky stated that the Trustees have fully caught up with the accountings and the tax returns, that he is ready, willing and able to sit and discuss the matter, that there is no problem with providing what they have been paid out of the joint account and that details of the partnership management compensation can be provided. The Court suggested issuing an order setting out further dates on the petitions. The Parties are to meet and confer with all of principals and professionals about all of the requests for information and responses by December 4, 2017. Further additional information is to be provided by November 3, 2017. The Petition to Suspend and Remove Louis DeMartini and Stephen DeMartini as Co-Trustees, etc. and the Petition for Approval of Co-Trustees Accounts and Reports, etc. are continued to January 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Skootsky will prepare a form of order and provide it to Mr. Colopy.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Co-Trustees Accounts And Status Reports For Calendar Years 2011 Through 2015

Petition For Order Requiring Co-Trustees To Perform Accounting And Produce Status Report And Records And Exhibits A-G
Off Calendar May-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Colopy for and with Mark Lewis (Petitioner).
Seth Skootksy for Louis Demartini and Stephen Demartini (Co-Trustees).
John Minton appeared by telephone for Laurie Low (Beneficiary).

Mr. Colopy stated that there has been a steady decrease in communication and performance of the assets in the past five years, that salaries have gone up in the past three years, that they are not generating financial statements or K-1's, and that there requests for information have been ignored. Mr. Colopy also stated that he would like an accounting within
60-days, that this has been going on for a long time and that the annual status report has not been addressed. Mr. Skootksy stated that the reports are for the general partnership joint account, that they have been in regular contact with the Accountant and CPA, that the 2014/2015 reports will be prepared by the end of the month and properly distributed, and that this is needed before the Trust accounting. Mr. Skootsky also stated that the Trust is the next level, that 2011, 2012 and 2013 have also been requested which they can not commit to, that they can commit to 2014 and 2015, that 2016 is extended and suggested a status hearing in July. The Court ordered that the accountings through 2015 be completed by July 11, 2017 and stated that it expects the Trustees will have shared as much information as they can. The Parties are to meet and confer as to any more specific things that they want and be responsive. All other issues are reserved for further resolution and are to be included in the status reports. Mr. Colopy stated that he wants the accountings to be approved. The Court granted the Petition for Order Requiring Co-Trustees to Perform Accountings, etc. (subject to receipt and review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
